prompt: The loop prints one extra line. What is the bug?
choices: The for loop condition should use < 5 instead of <= 5
The separator println at the end is outside the loop and always executes, adding one extra separator after the last line
if (i < 5) should be if (i <= 5) to print all separators
System.out.println cannot be called after a for loop
explanation: The extra System.out.println("--- (extra)") after the loop runs unconditionally, printing one more separator. The fence-post pattern (N items, N-1 separators) was correctly implemented inside the loop. The extra line after the loop is the bug — remove it.
